# UC-22.1.25 · GDPR Security Incident Tracking Linked to Personal Data Impact

## Description

Article 32(1)(c) requires the ability to restore availability and access in a timely manner, and incident handling underpins the integrity and resilience of processing. This use case tags security incidents with personal-data impact classification, tracks containment and eradication milestones, and aligns with breach notification workflows — extending generic SOC metrics with GDPR-specific impact labels.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enrich notables with asset lookups that flag personal-data processing; (2) require analysts to set `status_description` with PII impact keywords when known; (3) alert DPO workflow when `CONFIRMED_PII`; (4) correlate with UC-22.1.3 for 72h authority notification clocks; (5) retain closed-incident exports for accountability.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `gdpr_system_access_matrix.csv` (personal data flags), ServiceNow incident exports.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enrich notables with asset lookups that flag personal-data processing; (2) require analysts to set `status_description` with PII impact keywords when known; (3) alert DPO workflow when `CONFIRMED_PII`; (4) correlate with UC-22.1.3 for 72h authority notification clocks; (5) retain closed-incident exports for accountability.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` earliest=-14d
| lookup gdpr_system_access_matrix.csv system_hostname AS dest OUTPUT data_classification
| eval gdpr_impact=case(
    isnotnull(data_classification) AND match(lower(status_description),"(?i)pii|personal|breach|exfil"), "CONFIRMED_PII",
    isnotnull(data_classification), "POTENTIAL_PII_SYSTEM",
    1=1, "UNKNOWN")
| stats latest(status) as status latest(owner) as owner max(_time) as last_event by rule_name, gdpr_impact
| where gdpr_impact IN ("CONFIRMED_PII","POTENTIAL_PII_SYSTEM")
| table rule_name, gdpr_impact, status, owner, last_event
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Security Incident Tracking Linked to Personal Data Impact (Art. 32(1)(c))** — Article 32(1)(c) requires the ability to restore availability and access in a timely manner, and incident handling underpins the integrity and resilience of processing. This use case tags security incidents with personal-data impact classification, tracks containment and eradication milestones, and aligns with breach notification workflows — extending generic SOC metrics with GDPR-specific impact labels.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `gdpr_system_access_matrix.csv` (personal data flags), ServiceNow incident exports. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gdpr_impact** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **rule_name, gdpr_impact**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where gdpr_impact IN ("CONFIRMED_PII","POTENTIAL_PII_SYSTEM")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: gdpr_impact, last_event, owner, rule_name, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample results with the ES Incident Review queue and analyst outcomes so counts, severities, and fields match what your security operations team triages in production.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (GDPR-tagged incidents), Single value (open PII-related incidents), Timeline (milestone progression), Pie chart (impact classification).

## SPL

```spl
`notable` earliest=-14d
| lookup gdpr_system_access_matrix.csv system_hostname AS dest OUTPUT data_classification
| eval gdpr_impact=case(
    isnotnull(data_classification) AND match(lower(status_description),"(?i)pii|personal|breach|exfil"), "CONFIRMED_PII",
    isnotnull(data_classification), "POTENTIAL_PII_SYSTEM",
    1=1, "UNKNOWN")
| stats latest(status) as status latest(owner) as owner max(_time) as last_event by rule_name, gdpr_impact
| where gdpr_impact IN ("CONFIRMED_PII","POTENTIAL_PII_SYSTEM")
| table rule_name, gdpr_impact, status, owner, last_event
```

## Visualization

Table (GDPR-tagged incidents), Single value (open PII-related incidents), Timeline (milestone progression), Pie chart (impact classification). Key fields: `gdpr_impact`, `last_event`, `owner`, `rule_name`, `status`.
